title: 10 best herbal creams for shortness of breath

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain eucalyptus oil, which has natural decongestant properties that help to relieve respiratory issues. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing constriction and allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the lungs, further alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum creams can provide fast-acting relief from respiratory congestion and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate symptoms of shortness of breath and make it easier to breathe.

Bronchodilatory: The natural compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum, such as eucalyptol, help open up constricted airways and improve lung function, making it easier to take deep breaths and alleviating shortness of breath.

Antioxidant: The cream's antioxidant properties help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by pollutants, smoke, or other environmental irritants, which can contribute to shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues associated with shortness of breath.
Barks: The barks contain phenolic compounds that have been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, making it useful in reducing airway constriction and improving breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and dry them according to traditional methods for at least 30 minutes.
Combine 200 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 400 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for one hour and thirty minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix 20 percent of the infused oil with beeswax and coconut oil, then whip until creamy and smooth for approximately 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called carnosic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways. When applied topically, sage creams can help calm and soothe the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, sage's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out mucus and congestion, allowing for more efficient airflow and improved lung function.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Herbal sage creams help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion and wheezing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, allowing for smoother breathing and increased oxygen intake.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots help soothe bronchial constriction and reduce inflammation associated with shortness of breath.
Bark: The bitter principles present in sage bark help relax respiratory muscles, ease breathing difficulty, and combat respiratory infections that may exacerbate shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them in a warm place for at least two weeks to preserve potency.
Combine one tablespoon of dried salvia officinalis leaves with eight ounces of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then remove from heat and let it cool before straining off the liquid through a cheesecloth.
Mix the extracted herbal oil with an equal amount of beeswax and melt them together in a double boiler set over boiling water.
Pour the melted wax and herbal oil mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for about two hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with shortness of breath because its active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, exhibit bronchodilatory properties. These natural ingredients help relax airway muscles, reducing constriction and inflammation that can contribute to breathing difficulties. The antimicrobial properties of thyme also help combat respiratory infections, which can exacerbate shortness of breath. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects on the skin may also provide relief from symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as wheezing and coughing.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a natural antiseptic and antibacterial compound found in thyme, helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Cineole: Cineole, a monoterpene present in thyme, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which can help relax airway passages and improve breathing.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, another monoterpene found in thyme, has decongestant properties that can help reduce mucus buildup in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and shortness of breath.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation in the airways and alleviating respiratory distress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems from a trusted source or store-dried thymus vulgaris in a cool dark place.
Combine 50 grams of dried thymus vulgaris with 1000 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least four weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted then pour into lip balm tubes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the skin, this compound can help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, making it easier for the body to breathe. Additionally, licorice creams may also help to relax the bronchial muscles, allowing for more efficient airflow into the lungs, which can be particularly helpful for individuals with asthma or other respiratory conditions that cause shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Reduces inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate breathing difficulties by relaxing airway constriction and decreasing mucus production.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and lung damage, helping to improve overall lung function and reduce shortness of breath.

Phenolic acids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax airway muscles, reduce bronchospasm, and alleviate symptoms of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots are used to make medicinal creams for shortness of breath due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe respiratory tract irritation.
Barks: Licorice barks contain flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to improve lung function and reduce inflammation in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in quantities sufficient for the recipe and process them accordingly.
Combine 20 grams of processed glycyrrhiza glabra root powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids and set it aside to cool completely.
Melt 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and slowly add the cooled liquid extract stirring constantly until fully incorporated.
Whip the mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency filling air-tight containers for storage and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with shortness of breath because the menthol content in peppermint can help to relax the airways, making it easier to breathe. The cream can be applied topically to the chest and back, where it is absorbed into the skin and enters the bloodstream. From there, it can reach the lungs and help to open up airways that may be constricted due to allergy or other respiratory issues, providing fast and effective relief from shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Relaxation Inducing Properties: Peppermint creams can help relax the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing shortness of breath due to their calming effect on the respiratory system.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The menthol in peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways, which can constrict breathing passages and cause shortness of breath.

Expectorant Properties: Peppermint's natural expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating shortness of breath caused by congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain high amounts of menthol, which helps to ease respiratory congestion and relieve symptoms of shortness of breath.
Barks: Peppermint bark has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma, making it a popular choice for medicinal creams for shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the early morning and rinse them gently under cool running water.
Steam distill the peppermint leaves using a copper still or a steam distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Combine 10% peppermint essential oil with 90% sweet almond oil and heat it slowly to 120°f over low heat.
Whip the oil mixture vigorously with an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency in about 5 minutes.
Fill sterilized tubes or jars with the peppermint cream and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that has been shown to improve respiratory function by increasing blood flow and oxygenation to the lungs. The anti-inflammatory properties of rosemary also help to reduce constriction in the airways, making it easier for people to breathe. Additionally, rosemary's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, further alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate breathing difficulties by allowing airways to open up more easily.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in rosemary cream loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the airways, which can help relax bronchial muscles and improve airflow, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots can help soothe and calm respiratory issues associated with shortness of breath.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain flavonoids that have been shown to improve lung function and reduce inflammation, making them a key ingredient in creams for alleviating shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when they are most potent and fragrant.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 180°f for two hours or until crumbly.
Combine one part dried rosmarinus officinalis with five parts coconut oil and three parts beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for one hour, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 180°f.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature before using within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The herbal ingredients in these creams, such as echinacea, work to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the creams can also help to thin mucus, allowing it to be more easily cleared from the lungs, which can further alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal Kansas coneflower creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier for oxygen to reach the body's tissues.
Expectorant properties: Kansas coneflower's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and reducing wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower plant contain compounds that help to relax airway muscles, reducing congestion and improving breathing.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root and crush it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the crushed echinacea powder with 100 grams of organic coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 120 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of lavender essential oil to the warm mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the final cream into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature before use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax and open up the airways, allowing for easier breathing. The cream's active ingredients, such as fennel essential oil, work to reduce swelling in the lungs and bronchial passages, making it simpler for oxygen to pass through. This can be especially beneficial for individuals who suffer from asthma or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), providing a natural alternative to pharmaceutical medications.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and reduced congestion, which can alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath, asthma, and other respiratory conditions.

Bronchodilatory property: The volatile oils present in fennel, particularly anethole, have a bronchodilating effect, which means they can help relax and dilate the airway passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help to alleviate respiratory issues and ease breathing.
Barks: The bark of the fennel plant contains expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers.
Combine the foeniculum vulgare with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow the active compounds to dissolve.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely at room temperature before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The herb's potent flavonoids and phenolic acids help to relax the airways, reducing constriction and inflammation caused by asthma, allergies, or other conditions. This natural cream also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, which can alleviate congestion and wheezing associated with shortness of breath. As a result, pot marigold creams provide natural relief from respiratory discomforts, allowing for easier breathing and improved overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and constriction in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to shortness of breath.
Expectorant properties: The saponins in pot marigold can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain calcium, magnesium, and potassium which help to relax the airways and reduce inflammation, providing relief from shortness of breath.
Seeds: The seeds of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and improving respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at dawn or early morning when their potency is highest.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a warm, dry, dark place for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 20% dried calendula flowers with 80% coconut oil and gently heat the mixture until it reaches 160°f.
Remove from heat and let the mixture steep at room temperature for 2-3 hours to allow the calendula to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids, then store the infused oil in an amber glass bottle for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain the calming properties of lavender, which have a profound impact on respiratory health. The cream's active ingredients work to soothe and relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constricting of bronchial tubes, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, lavender's natural sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, further alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath by promoting relaxation and reducing stress-related respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath by allowing for easier breathing.

Calming and sedative properties: The calming effects of lavender, particularly its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, can help alleviate feelings of panic and discomfort associated with shortness of breath, making it easier to breathe naturally.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds may help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals and infection, which can contribute to chronic respiratory conditions like bronchitis or pneumonia that can cause shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
Buds: Lavender buds contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 3 days.
Combine the ground lavandula angustifolia powder with 200ml of coconut oil and heat it in a double boiler at 65°c for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and allowing the mixture to cool to room temperature within 30 minutes.
Add 50g of beeswax pellets to the cooled oil mixture and heat it in a double boiler at 80°c for an additional 15 minutes until the wax is fully incorporated.
Remove the cream from heat and let it cool and thicken completely within 2 hours before transferring it to glass jars with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tickseed creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain a unique combination of herbs that work synergistically to relax the airways and reduce inflammation. The herbs, such as mullein and lobelia, have a soothing effect on the lungs and bronchial tubes, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, tickseed creams can help to thin mucus, allowing for better airflow and relieving congestion. This natural combination of herbs helps to ease shortness of breath, providing relief for those who struggle with respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Ginger: Ginger has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate respiratory issues and reduce inflammation in the lungs, which could potentially ease shortness of breath.

Eucalyptus: Eucalyptus oil is commonly used in natural remedies for respiratory issues due to its decongestant and bronchodilatory properties, which can help relieve congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe.

Marshmallow root: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory and mucilaginous properties that may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tickseed plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the lungs and bronchial tubes.
Buds: Tickseed buds contain flavonoids, which have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and improve lung function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphorbia hirta leaves and stems, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pests.
Dry the harvested plant material using a clean towel or air drying rack for at least 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried euphorbia hirta with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or thermometer-controlled water bath at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding solids and storing the resulting cream in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain the essential oil of lemon verbena, which has a natural relaxing effect on the bronchial passages. The oil's calming properties help to soothe and open up constricted airways, making it easier for oxygen to flow in and out of the lungs. This relief can provide significant comfort to individuals who struggle with shortness of breath due to conditions such as chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), asthma, or bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena creams reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath caused by conditions such as bronchitis or asthma.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in lemon verbena help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with shortness of breath.
Antispasmodic properties: Lemon verbena's antispasmodic effects can help relax airway muscles and reduce spasms that can contribute to shortness of breath, improving overall lung function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve lung function.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried verbena officinalis leaves measuring 10 grams and infuse them in boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard solids.
Mix 50 milliliters of the infused liquid with 150 milliliters of coconut oil and let it cool down to room temperature.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil until well combined and smooth in texture.
Blend the cooled mixture from step 3 with the whipped beeswax mixture until a thick creamy consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with shortness of breath because its natural compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, have been shown to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the airways. This relief can help alleviate symptoms of asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and other respiratory conditions that cause difficulty breathing. By reducing spasms and swelling in the airway, herbal lemon balm creams may provide quick and effective relief from shortness of breath, allowing individuals to breathe easier and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Lemon balm's antispasmodic property helps to relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can exacerbate shortness of breath.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of lemon balm cream may help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to chronic respiratory issues such as asthma or COPD.
Anxiolytic: Lemon balm's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related breathing difficulties, such as hyperventilation or rapid shallow breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain compounds that help relax airways and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating shortness of breath.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers are rich in flavonoids, which have a bronchodilatory effect, helping to open up airways and improve breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source, measuring 20 grams for the recipe.
Combine the dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of organic sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler set at medium heat for 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids after 15 minutes.
Mix 50 milliliters of the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain bioactive compounds that have a profound effect on respiratory health. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger cream help to relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction caused by conditions like bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, the warming sensation provided by the cream can help to loosen mucus, making it easier to breathe. This natural relief can bring comfort and ease to individuals experiencing shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath caused by conditions like bronchitis or asthma.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of ginger help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion that may be contributing to shortness of breath.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginger's vasodilatory effects can help dilate blood vessels in the lungs, improving oxygenation and reducing feelings of shortness of breath by increasing blood flow to the respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving respiratory issues like shortness of breath.
Roots: Ginger roots are used to make teas, tinctures, and creams that can help alleviate bronchial spasms and reduce inflammation in the airways, providing relief for people with shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh ginger roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the ginger roots in a warm oven at 60°c for 12 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Grind 100g of dried ginger into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20% of the powdered ginger with 80% coconut oil and 5% beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes to ensure the ingredients are fully incorporated and cooled to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams help with shortness of breath because they contain curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways. This can help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), which often cause shortness of breath. By reducing inflammation and opening up air passages, turmeric creams can improve lung function and make it easier to breathe, providing relief from shortness of breath and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's potent anti-inflammatory compound curcumin helps reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Bronchodilatory: The active compounds in turmeric, such as curcumin and turmerone, have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and increase airflow, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate bronchial inflammation and reduces airway constriction.
Roots: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, roots have been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
remedy_recipe: Grate one teaspoon of dried turmeric root into a small bowl containing two tablespoons of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over medium heat for 10 minutes until it reaches 100°f.
Add five drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine with the turmeric-infused oil.
Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature before whipping with an electric mixer for two minutes.
Transfer the whipped cream to a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to seven days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain a unique combination of natural ingredients that work synergistically to relax the airways and improve lung function. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing, while its expectorant properties help loosen mucus and promote coughing up impurities. Additionally, the cream's antioxidants help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, further supporting respiratory health and reducing symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Passionflower: Passionflower helps to calm the nervous system, which can contribute to shortness of breath by reducing anxiety and stress-induced respiratory issues.
Marshmallow root: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs, helping to reduce inflammation and ease breathing difficulties.
Lavender: Lavender oil has a calming effect on the respiratory system, which can help to relax bronchial muscles and improve airflow, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate bronchial constriction and relieve shortness of breath.
Fruits: Contain a high concentration of vitamin C, which has antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and ease respiratory distress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passionflower flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Mix one cup of dried flowers with two cups of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily, allowing the oil to infuse.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding solids after 24 hours.
Whip the infused oil with one tablespoon of beeswax and two teaspoons of coconut oil until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Betony creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain the natural herb Stachys officinalis, which has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. The cream's anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties help to reduce congestion and ease breathing, making it an effective remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). By soothing the airways and reducing inflammation, betony creams can help to relieve the tightness and discomfort associated with shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Betony's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath by allowing for easier airflow and reducing bronchial constriction.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of betony help to relax bronchial muscles, which can become constricted during an asthma attack or other respiratory conditions, leading to shortness of breath.

Expectorant property: Betony's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for the body to expel and reducing the feeling of shortness of breath caused by excess mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Betony roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate respiratory issues associated with shortness of breath.
Barks: The barks of the betony plant contain compounds that have been traditionally used to soothe bronchial inflammation, making it an effective ingredient for calming breathing difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried stachys officinalis leaves from reputable suppliers in amounts of 20 grams for every 100ml cream base.
Combine the dried stachys officinalis leaves with 70% isopropyl alcohol in a ratio of 1:5 and steep for 24 hours to create a tincture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes of sedimentation has occurred.
Mix 20% w/w beeswax and 80% w/w coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth, then cool to 65°c for adding the tincture.
Combine the cooled wax-oil mixture with the stachys officinalis tincture at a ratio of 1:3 and stir thoroughly before pouring into sterile glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain the herb valerian root, which has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. The cream is absorbed through the skin, where it works to soothe respiratory passages and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe. By calming the body's response to stress and anxiety, valerian creams help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath, providing relief from conditions such as asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and sleep apnea.
remedy_properties: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a primary active compound in valerian root, helps to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to improved airflow and reduced shortness of breath.

Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, another key component of valerian, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that may help to calm bronchial spasms and open up airways, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.

Valerin: Valerin, a volatile oil found in valerian root, has been shown to exhibit expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, which can help to alleviate congestion and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of volatile compounds, including sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which help to relax the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways and relieve bronchial constriction, making them useful for treating shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valeriana officinalis root extract measuring 2 tablespoons and crush it into fine powder.
Combine 4 ounces of beeswax, 6 ounces of coconut oil, and 2 ounces of olive oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Add the crushed valeriana officinalis powder to the mixture and stir well for approximately 10 minutes until smooth consistency achieved.
Transfer the mixture to a double boiler or a heat-proof glass measuring cup placed over simmering water and stir occasionally for 20-30 minutes until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for about an hour before transferring it to an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for shortness of breath are topical remedies that combine natural herbs with moisturizing ingredients to provide relief from respiratory distress. These creams work by soothing and calming irritated airways, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Examples of herbal creams that help with shortness of breath include those containing eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme. By applying these creams to the chest or neck, individuals can experience improved breathing, reduced wheezing, and enhanced overall lung function. This natural relief enables people to participate in daily activities without feeling winded or fatigued, improving their quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat shortness of breath with herbal creams is to apply a topical cream containing ginger, eucalyptus, and peppermint essential oils. These herbs help relax bronchial passages, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion. For optimal results, gently massage the cream onto your chest, back, and shoulders before bedtime to allow for deep relaxation and improved breathing during sleep.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shortness of breath that are treated with herbal creams are respiratory conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint, eucalyptus, and thyme may help relieve congestion, ease coughing, and open airways. Other conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and rosacea that cause skin irritation and itching may also be treated with herbal creams, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healthy skin.
